evals.prompt: I just ran /validate-benchmark on my React project and got a score of 68/100 (Grade: D). The breakdown is: Coverage 50, Evidence Quality 80, Enforcement 60, Speed 95. What does this mean? What should I fix first?
evals.expected_output: Breakdown: You have low coverage (≤2 journeys validated) and low enforcement (hooks not installed, maybe some test files present). Fix in this order: (1) Coverage first — you only validated login and home page. Add journeys for create, edit, delete, error cases. Each journey adds evidence quality and validates more features. (2) Enforcement — install missing hooks (.claude/hooks/), add rules files, remove any *.test.* or *.spec.* files from src/. (3) Evidence Quality is already good (80), so focus on quantity (more journeys) and discipline (hooks). Rerun benchmark in 1 week — target: 75+ grade (B range). Example: 5 journeys = 85 coverage, 100 evidence quality, 80 enforcement = 87 grade (B).

evals.prompt: Our team has been validating a fullstack app. Last week's benchmark was 92/100 (Grade: A). This week it's 76/100 (Grade: C). The breakdown: Coverage 70 (was 95), Evidence Quality 60 (was 100), others stable. What happened? Why did our score drop so much?
evals.expected_output: You've lost coverage (fewer journeys) and evidence quality (more empty/junk files). Likely causes: (1) Someone deleted journey directories from e2e-evidence/ without re-running validation. (2) New evidence files were captured but are mostly empty (zero-byte placeholders). (3) Validation wasn't re-run this week, so old evidence is considered stale. Action: (1) Re-run validation with all original journeys + any new ones. (2) Delete empty/junk files from e2e-evidence/. (3) Check if a verdict report was generated (bonus +30 on quality). Re-benchmark — should be back to 92+ if you've captured fresh evidence.
